w:r.w:t: PromptHouse Antigravity Autonomous Studio Blueprint v0.1

Turn PromptHouse into an autonomous smart prompt-engineering and prompt-to-app studio that can be built in Google Antigravity or a similar agentic IDE, while preserving PromptHouse logic, Max Lock, proof gates, and lawful connector boundaries.
Prompt Architect, Product Framer, Shell Designer, Tool Router
Canon Keeper, Workflow Smith, Artifact Builder, Truth Auditor, Launch Marshal, Governance Sentinel
product architecture, Antigravity mission prompt, connector handshake protocol, autonomy model, MVP scope, data model, and implementation route
aligned to PromptHouse self-build law and boundary law
; every bridge requires permission, credentials, API surface, user approval, or a local adapter
build the MVP as a web-first app with mobile shell, then add native mobile after the prompt-to-app loop is stable
Pons vera. Bridge only through lawful handshakes.
Autonomous smart prompt-engineering + prompt-to-app/mobile engineering studio.
User gives intent. PromptHouse turns it into:
asks for approval before risky actions
hidden background operation beyond the actual platform
Purpose: user starts projects and sees all agents, tasks, risks, and artifacts.
Purpose: turns rough user prompts into governed, reusable, tested prompts.
Purpose: turns a prompt or product idea into software design and implementation tasks.
Purpose: manages handshake connectors to tools, APIs, files, local apps, and workflows.
Purpose: replaces fake completion with verifiable artifacts.
intake → canon check → route → spec → build plan → connector check → execute/propose → test → repair → proof → handoff
The app must preserve the 11 internal modules:
Use public bot identities as optional UI/personality wrappers:
by force. It handshakes with systems that expose a permissioned surface.
"oauth | api_key | openapi | mcp | webhook | cli | file | browser | database | human_handoff"
discover → authenticate → scope → live-run → user approve → execute → capture proof → rollback path → log
Require special approval for deletes, payments, emails, deployments, database writes, or external messages.
Deny destructive shell commands by default.
Never place API keys in generated prompt text.
Log what was attempted, what succeeded, and what failed.
Provide a rollback path before risky execution.
Studio only drafts plans and prompts.
Studio generates specs, files, tests, and handoffs.
Studio reads approved sources and summarizes proof.
Studio proposes writes, pull requests, issues, messages, or deployments, but user approves every action.
Studio can execute low-risk preapproved actions within allowlists.
Only for safe workspaces with strong allowlists, backups, rollback, and audit artifacts.
Backend: Node/TypeScript API routes or FastAPI
Auth: Supabase Auth, Clerk, or Firebase Auth
File storage: Supabase Storage or Google Cloud Storage
Jobs: background worker queue when available
Connector registry: database-backed JSON connector contracts
Expo / React Native shell consuming the same backend
Swift/Kotlin only if performance, OS integration, or enterprise device policy requires it
"intake | planning | building | testing | repaired | shipped | blocked"
"plan | diff | screenshot | recording | test_report | receipt | handoff"
Build me a mobile app for X.
Paste this into Antigravity as the first mission.
MISSION: Build PromptHouse Evo Studio OS MVP

You are building PromptHouse Evo Studio OS: an autonomous smart prompt-engineering and prompt-to-app studio. The app must transform user intent into governed prompts, app specs, mobile/web engineering plans, connector handshakes, tests, proof artifacts, and deployment handoffs.
Create a web-first MVP with a mobile-ready architecture. It must include Mission Control, Prompt Forge, App Forge, Bridge Hub, and Proof Deck.
intake → canon check → route → spec → build plan → connector check → execute/propose → test → repair → proof → handoff
known, inferred, blocked, broken, built, verified, recommended.
1. Mission Control — mission board, module route, status, artifacts, tests, risks.
2. Prompt Forge — prompt intake, rewrite, constraints, variables, tests, versioning.
3. App Forge — product brief, user stories, screens, data model, API contracts, code tasks.
4. Bridge Hub — connector registry with permissioned handshakes.
5. Proof Deck — plans, diffs, screenshots, test reports, receipts, handoffs.

Do not build uncontrolled access to “anything.” Build a Universal Handshake Layer that supports OAuth, API keys, OpenAPI, MCP, webhooks, CLI adapters, browser automation, databases, and file import/export. All connectors must declare capabilities, scopes, risk level, approval policy, input/output schema, audit artifacts, and rollback strategy.
Level 3: Draft Actions. The system can plan and propose writes, pull requests, deployments, or messages, but user approval is required before execution.
- Special approval for delete, payment, email/message send, production deploy, database write, or destructive shell command.
- Maintain audit logs and proof artifacts.
- Provide rollback strategy before risky actions.

Use Next.js + React + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui for the MVP frontend. Use a typed backend API, Supabase/Postgres for persistence, and a connector registry stored as JSON contracts. Keep the architecture ready for Expo/React Native mobile shell later.
Create implementation plans, code diffs, test outputs, and screenshots where possible. Do not mark deployment complete unless it is actually deployed and verified.
If any requested capability is blocked by permissions, credentials, platform limits, or missing APIs, label it blocked and implement the strongest lawful substitute.
create audit log for connector actions
Truth states only use allowed labels.
Ship status cannot be set without proof artifact.
Destructive action is blocked by default.
API key is never displayed in prompt output.
Proof Deck logs every connector action.
Mission Control shows active route and next action.
Prompt Forge shows raw and compiled prompt.
App Forge shows screens, data model, and code tasks.
Bridge Hub shows connector risk and approval policy.
Proof Deck shows artifact status and verification note.
Mobile viewport keeps approval queue usable.
requires special approval and rollback plan.
is limited to actual database/project storage.
Prompt Forge creates a governed PromptSpec
App Forge creates a basic AppSpec
module route and truth states are visible
README explains install, run, test, and deploy
Add AI-native capabilities that reduce dev time for vibe coders, AI coders, solo builders, and engineering teams.
The studio should not merely generate code. It should compress the entire dev loop:
idea → spec → scaffold → code → test → preview → repair → PR/deploy → proof
Purpose: let a nontraditional builder describe the feel, look, product idea, or desired outcome and quickly receive working app structure.
accepts messy natural language, voice-style notes, screenshots, examples, inspiration links, and rough ideas
extracts product intent, target user, desired feel, platform, must-have features, and constraints
converts the vibe into a product brief, user stories, screen map, data model, and build plan
asks only high-impact questions; fills low-risk gaps with labeled assumptions
produces repo structure, starter screens, route map, component plan, data objects, and first task list
turns design inspiration into a style system: tone, color direction, typography class, spacing, interaction feel, and component vocabulary
never copies protected branding directly unless the user owns it or asks for a non-infringing inspired direction
outputs screen-by-screen user flows before code begins
supports mobile-first, desktop-first, or responsive-first paths
gives step-by-step build instructions for users who do not code deeply
converts technical work into clear builder actions
Purpose: help experienced AI coders move faster with stronger context, better tickets, safer diffs, and less debugging.
scans a repo or uploaded file tree
builds a map of architecture, routes, components, APIs, data models, tests, build scripts, and risk areas
creates compact context bundles for AI coding agents
includes relevant files, constraints, coding style, acceptance tests, edge cases, and forbidden changes
converts vague tasks into GitHub-ready issues with scope, files likely affected, acceptance criteria, tests, risk, and rollback
decomposes a feature into parallel lanes: frontend, backend, database, tests, docs, deployment, QA
prevents overlap by assigning file ownership and merge order
reviews diffs for regressions, security, style drift, architecture violations, missing tests, and unclear changes
detects missing unit, integration, E2E, accessibility, and smoke tests
turns a bug report into reproduction steps, failing test, suspected files, and repair plan
maps dependency impact before a refactor
creates phased migrations instead of risky rewrites
proposes package upgrades, breaking-change notes, test plan, and rollback steps
generates commit messages, PR summaries, reviewer notes, screenshots requested, and test evidence
Purpose: make PromptHouse the prompt brain for app builders.
catches vague roles, missing inputs, unclear output format, conflicting constraints, unsafe tool assumptions, and untestable success criteria
generates test prompts and expected output checks for every production prompt
compares multiple prompt versions against the same cases
tracks win/loss reasons and regression notes
stores reusable prompt patterns, variables, style rules, user preferences, and project constraints in real project storage
never claims unsupported memory outside the actual app database
turns a prompt into a structured tool schema, input validation, output validation, and error states
recommends model class by task: cheap/fast, reasoning-heavy, code-heavy, vision, long-context, or local/private
logs recommendation as inferred unless benchmarked in the app
compresses files, history, specs, and logs into token-efficient context packs
separates public user prompts, internal instructions, secrets, connector configs, and tool permissions
Purpose: shorten the path from working feature to verified deployment.
starts dev server through approved terminal action
records page load, navigation errors, visual breakage, form failures, and accessibility issues
checks env vars, build command, tests, migrations, secrets, domain, auth, and rollback plan
defines revert commit, DB rollback, feature flag disable, package downgrade, or manual recovery path
turns merged work into user-facing release notes, changelog, and internal technical notes
proposes events, logs, error tracking, funnels, and performance metrics
wraps risky features behind flags and staged rollout plans
Purpose: help users connect the studio to real tools faster while staying permissioned.
asks what tool the user wants to connect
identifies likely handshake type: OAuth, API key, MCP, OpenAPI, webhook, CLI, browser, file, database, or human handoff
creates typed client stubs, connector contract, test calls, and permission summary
generates a starter MCP server for a tool, database, file set, or custom workflow
exposes tools/resources/prompts using explicit permission gates
creates trigger/action flows for tools like Slack, Discord, GitHub, Notion, Linear, Airtable, or custom services
stores keys only in real secret storage or environment variables
never embeds secrets in prompts, screenshots, logs, or exported docs
live-runs connector actions without writing data
generates request/response previews and risk notes
shows pending writes, deploys, emails, database changes, payments, or destructive commands
supports approve, reject, edit, require rollback, or ask for more proof
Purpose: support solo builders, vibe-coding teams, and engineering teams.
quick fix, feature, refactor, migration, integration, risky change, architecture change
flags when an agent drifts outside the issue or touches unapproved files
creates done checks from feature intent
produces reviewer-ready packet: summary, files changed, screenshots, test evidence, risks, rollback, and open questions
explains why code was generated, what each file does, and how to modify it
prepares work for a human developer, contractor, or coding agent
Raw idea creates a product brief without over-questioning.
Vibe terms are translated into concrete UI and product constraints.
and receive a specific design-system patch.
Repo Cartographer identifies routes, components, APIs, tests, and build scripts.
Context Pack Builder includes only relevant files and constraints.
Issue-to-PR Compiler produces acceptance criteria and likely files touched.
Multi-Agent Work Splitter avoids assigning the same files to conflicting agents.
Diff Critic catches missing tests and risky architecture changes.
Prompt Linter catches missing output format.
Prompt Unit Tests include edge cases and failure cases.
Prompt Eval Runner compares prompt versions using the same cases.
Tool contract output validates inputs and outputs.
Context Budget Optimizer reduces context while preserving critical constraints.
Browser QA Agent logs console errors and failed navigation.
CI Failure Explainer identifies the likely failing file or command.
Deployment Readiness Gate blocks missing environment variables.
Rollback Planner exists before high-risk deploy.
Release Notes Generator separates user-facing and technical notes.
OpenAPI import creates connector contract and client stub plan.
MCP builder creates permissioned tool/resource/prompt design.
Secrets are never emitted into prompt text.
Approval Queue requires explicit consent for write/deploy/delete/payment/message actions.
Paste this into Antigravity after the base MVP prompt.
MISSION ADDENDUM: Add AI dev acceleration features for vibe coders and AI coders.

Expand PromptHouse Evo Studio OS with an AI Dev Acceleration Layer. The product must speed up the loop from idea to working app by supporting vibe intake, prompt engineering, code task generation, repo context, connector setup, QA, review packets, and deployment readiness.
1. Vibe Fast Lane — messy idea to product brief, style DNA, screen storyboard, scaffold plan.
2. AI Coder Console — repo cartography, context packs, issue-to-PR compiler, multi-agent work splitter, diff critic.
3. Prompt Intelligence Lab — prompt linter, prompt tests, eval runner, model router advisor, context budget optimizer.
4. QA Autopilot — browser QA, bug reproduction, CI failure explainer, test gap finder.
5. Connector Wizard — OpenAPI importer, MCP server builder, webhook recipes, connector simulator, approval queue.
6. Review & Ship Deck — review packets, deployment gate, rollback planner, release notes, telemetry and feature flag planner.
- TypeScript interfaces for VibeSpec, RepoMap, ContextPack, AgentTask, PromptEval, ConnectorContract, ApprovalRequest, QAReport, ReviewPacket, DeploymentGate.
- UI mock screens for Vibe Fast Lane, AI Coder Console, Prompt Intelligence Lab, QA Autopilot, Connector Wizard, and Review & Ship Deck.
- Stub services that transform sample data through: rough idea → VibeSpec → AppSpec → AgentTasks → QAReport → ReviewPacket.
- Add approval gating for write/deploy/delete/payment/message actions.
- Add sample OpenAPI connector import and sample MCP connector design.
- A vibe coder can enter a rough idea and receive product brief, screen map, style DNA, and scaffold plan.
- An AI coder can generate an issue-to-PR packet with file targets, tests, risk, and rollback.
- A connector action cannot write externally without approval.
- Prompt linter catches missing variables, unclear output format, and unsafe tool assumptions.
- QA Autopilot produces a bug reproduction plan and test gap list.
- Review & Ship Deck blocks ship status without proof artifacts.

Do not implement fake connector access. Use mock connectors until credentials and real APIs are supplied. Do not store secrets in prompts or logs. Label every unimplemented integration as stubbed, blocked, or recommended.
18. INVENT / BUILD: Native Post-MVP Studio Package Complete

Invent and build PromptHouse Evo Native Studio as a post-MVP deployable reference package: mobile-first, prompt-architecture driven, compatible with Antigravity, Flutter, VS Code, Android Studio, OpenAPI/MCP, and any AI chat.
high-risk action blocking for external connection, deployment, deletion
required proof: owner approval, scope declaration, rollback path, proof receipt
seed prompts: Blueprint Architect, Prompt Fire Orders, Bot Customer Simulation
StudioMission, PromptRecord, FireOrder, Blueprint, ProofArtifact, BotCustomerFeedback
blocks production deployment claim until build, tests, signing, hosting, and release receipts exist
personas: Vibe Coder Founder, AI Coder Engineer, Mobile Freelancer, QA Reviewer
labeled simulation only, not real traction or revenue
portable operating prompt that lets the same studio logic run inside any AI chat
as a deployable reference package and mobile-first app scaffold.
truthfully production-deployed, app-store-published, cloud-hosted, revenue-validated, or real-customer-validated yet.
— install Flutter dependencies, run tests, and produce local run proof.
— add Supabase/Firebase auth, database, storage, saved missions, and real PromptBase persistence.
— Android/iOS signing, device tests, screenshots, and release proof.
